narrative.headline: 2020 Ford Escape: what owners complain about most
narrative.summary: The most-reported owner concerns for this model center on structural issues, most commonly interior fogging of headlamp covers. Powertrain complaints frequently describe persistent steering vibration and a low humming sound that intensifies at highway speeds. Electrical and fuel-system complaints round out the picture, with some fuel-system reports involving fires.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Owners report that headlamp covers develop foggy buildup on the interior surface, though illumination from the bulbs is generally described as adequate. · 1 crash · 12 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report a noticeable vibration in the steering that begins at lower speeds and persists through highway speeds, accompanied by a low humming sound that grows louder as speed increases. · 4 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report concerns about the vehicle's factory tow-package electrical configuration in relation to trailer braking requirements.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: Owners report unexpected hesitation during acceleration from a stop, sometimes accompanied by turn-signal indicator failure, with some incidents involving fire. · 1 fire

issues.title: Gasoline
issues.blurb: 111 complaints (8%), including 1 report of a fire. No crashes or injuries reported.
